title: How to safely transfer filler to a BD syringe. Avoid Cross Contamination!
filename: How to safely transfer filler to a BD syringe. Avoid Cross Contamination!.en.vtt
excerpt: Last week I did a video on BD syringes and why I use them and lots of people liked seeing that if you're in Australia you might want to click on and watch something else because I know you can't do this in Australia but you might be curious exactly what's going on in the rest of …

segments.text: last week I did a video on BD syringes and why I use them and lots of people liked seeing that if you're in Australia you might want to click on and watch something else because I know you can't do this in Australia but you might be curious exactly what's going on in the rest of the world this is how you transfer filler into the BD syringe because if you don't do this properly you end up with little bubbles you end up wasting product and it just makes for a Messier costlier situation so I thought I'd share with you how I get filler from the syringe that it comes in into the BD syringe safely first thing is obviously once you're taking the plunger off you want to make sure the plunger doesn't touch anything else so I just keep it separate I usually keep it in my hand but it's not touching anything else I then take the Villa syringe that I'm about to use and the important bit is that you don't slide this needle too far in because where this whole thing goes wrong is where you create spaces where there are bubbles that then create this kind of suspension feeling in the filler so as you you're

injecting you squash the air instead of squeezing the filler out we need as few Bubbles as possible and this technique has to be done in this way the other thing is we don't really want to fill this whole syringe with product because the needles go blunt I like to put in only a maximum of 0.1 mils into the back of the BD syringe before I use a different BD syringe so we're not filling the whole thing and using that as your device this enables you to create new ones and if you create a little bubble and you can't use it at least you haven't wasted the whole syringe because it is very hard injecting when there's a bubble so we want to slide the needle just into the start of the cylinder and then we start to inject and you should see a bubble of product form and while it's forming it's really important that the bevel stays in the middle of the bubble of the filler because that's how you minimize the bubbles if the bevel comes out of the filler you start to create little spaces which are your bubbles and I fill this until there's a little bulge of the product out the back of the syringe so it's like a little meniscus a

rounded shape of the filler and that's where this plunger is eventually going to connect so I will then place this the first thing the plunger touches is the filler that's how I get rid of the bubbles because now it's a completely perfect fit there's no room for any bubbles here and I can slide this slowly down to the other side of the syringe and I'm ready to inject without any bubbles in that space I hope you found that video helpful if you want to learn about why I use BD syringes and the advantages of using them along with some disadvantages I think then you can click the link on the video at the end of this show
